title: dianthus commutatus
intro: Dianthus commutatus, also known as Fringed Pink or Fringed Carnation, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink or white flowers with fringed petals. Traditionally, Dianthus commutatus has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and boosting the immune system. Dianthus commutatus can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
